note: Initial conservative triage. It prevents ceremonial Lean by distinguishing formal invariants from schema, process, and research targets.

triage_classes.formal-invariant: Near-term Lean or executable-model candidate after definitions are introduced.
triage_classes.schema-contract: Define JSON Schema or typed records first; proof comes after the contract exists.
triage_classes.process-contract: Requires governance/policy/event semantics or tests before proof is meaningful.
triage_classes.research-agenda: Tracked as an agenda item until narrowed into an operational predicate.

records.formal_target: A layer without external-action authority can only produce an action through an authorized handoff.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A handoff that exceeds the caller authority ceiling is rejected.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: Modeled layer-contract admission routes missing layer identity, lifecycle state, owner, responsibility, input artifacts, output artifacts, authority ceilings, handoff protocols, invariants, failure modes, evidence gates, external-action authority or handoff boundaries, source mappings, support-state boundaries, evidence-transition gaps, and non-claim-boundary gaps to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.StackBoundaries as a finite layer-contract admission route over explicit boundary, authority, handoff, source, support-state, evidence-transition, and non-claim records; it does not prove deployed layer enforcement, source-to-layer traceability completeness, runtime authority behavior, whole-system safety, support-state promotion, or model capability.

records.formal_target: A route is minimum viable only when no lower-cost authorized route satisfies the required quality predicate.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Efficiency as a finite route-comparison predicate: a minimum-viable route excludes listed lower-cost authorized candidates that satisfy the declared quality predicate.

records.formal_target: A routed or compressed result with open obligations cannot be promoted without a residual record.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Efficiency as a finite residual-promotion predicate: promoted work with open obligations must include a residual record.

records.formal_target: Modeled efficiency-claim admission routes missing task contracts, quality predicates, selected routes, candidate sets, lower-cost comparisons, cost ledgers, complete visible costs, verification results, failed quality, authority bypass, residual gaps, fallback gaps, hidden-cost audit gaps, benchmark or trace gaps, negative-control gaps, evidence-transition gaps, and non-claim-boundary gaps to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Efficiency as a finite efficiency-claim admission route over explicit accounting, verification, hidden-cost, evidence-transition, and non-claim-boundary records; it does not prove measured efficiency, route-search completeness, cost-estimate accuracy, route quality, benchmark performance, or support-state promotion.

records.formal_target: Every transition preserves or lowers the active authority ceiling unless a governance grant is present.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A missing grant blocks execution rather than becoming default authorization.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: Modeled authority lifecycle admission routes missing principals, operations, permission classes, caller ceilings, target requirements, delegation chains, grants, active grant state, expiry and revocation boundaries, scope matches, grant-ceiling coverage, approval records, effect or denial receipts, audit refs, evidence-transition records, and non-claim boundaries to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Authority as a finite authority lifecycle route over explicit principal, operation, permission, ceiling, delegation, grant, revocation, approval, receipt, audit, evidence-transition, and non-claim-boundary records; it does not prove deployed authorization enforcement, runtime adapter behavior, revocation propagation, approval-service quality, tool-wrapper security, or support-state promotion.

records.formal_target: A component with a failed required invariant cannot be promoted.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: An unbounded authority field is detected as a governance failure.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: Modeled failure recurrence and receipt review routes missing failure class, boundary, receipt, owner, containment, residual, learning path, normalization guard, review escalation, quarantine, evidence-transition, and non-claim-boundary records to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.FailureModes as a finite recurrence and failure-receipt route over explicit failure class, boundary, receipt, owner, containment, residual, learning-path, normalization-guard, review, quarantine, evidence-transition, and non-claim-boundary records; it does not prove runtime scenario coverage, evaluator independence, context containment, claim-verifier quality, recurrence detection, deployed failure detection, or support-state promotion.

records.formal_target: Support-state transitions require the corresponding evidence artifact.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A claim cannot be promoted when required evidence is absent.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A modeled support-state transition routes no-change requests, missing records, scope gaps, support-effect gaps, missing review, missing required evidence, terminal/downgrade gaps, changelog gaps, and missing non-claim boundaries to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.EvidenceStates as a finite transition-lifecycle route function and derived route theorems for no-change requests, missing claim records, scope-boundary gaps, support-effect gaps, support-effect mismatches, review gaps, missing required evidence, missing negative evidence, downgrade-trigger gaps, terminal-effect mismatches, missing changelog refs, missing non-claim boundaries, and complete transition acceptance.

records.formal_target: A compiled intent contract preserves declared constraints and stop conditions.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.IntentContracts as a finite compilation-review predicate: declared constraints and stop conditions are preserved by valid compilation.

records.formal_target: A contract missing required authority cannot compile to an executable job.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.IntentContracts as a finite authority-compilation predicate: missing required authority blocks executable-job compilation.

records.formal_target: Structured intent-resolution and intent-admission records route missing text, prohibited actions, ambiguity, conflicts, high-impact authority gaps, hidden overrides, preservation failures, re-contract triggers, and missing non-claim boundaries to rejection, clarification, repair, review, or re-contracting before compilation/admission.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.IntentContracts as finite resolution and admission route functions with derived route theorems for missing text, prohibited action, ambiguity, non-goal conflict, missing high-impact authority, irreversible high-impact review, hidden override rejection, unresolved ambiguity, constraint-precedence and preservation gaps, missing stop conditions, missing or widened authority, downstream re-contract triggers, missing non-claim boundaries, and complete admission.

records.formal_target: The synthetic intent-intake probe summary records four valid bounded-request scenarios, six expected-invalid request-pressure controls, stop-condition preservation, bounded-default non-authority, and no runtime-dispatch, support-state-promotion, or deployed-authority-extraction claim.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.IntentContracts as a finite summary bridge for python3 scripts/validate_intent_intake_probe.py; the bridge records the corpus counts, request-pressure negative controls, stop-condition and bounded-default checks, and explicit no-promotion boundaries.

records.formal_target: An admitted plan satisfies every active constitutional predicate.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Alignment as a finite constitutional-predicate review: an admitted plan marked against an active predicate must record predicate satisfaction.

records.formal_target: A self-modification that weakens a protected predicate is rejected.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Alignment as a finite self-modification predicate: weakening a protected predicate requires rejection.

records.formal_target: Modeled constitutional lifecycle admission routes missing predicate, source, operational-test, protected-scope, conflict-behavior, review, migration, self-modification, agency-rights, material-usability, pre-effect review, rollback, correction, reviewer-independence, evidence-transition, and non-claim-boundary records to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Alignment as a finite constitutional lifecycle route over predicate, normative-source, operational-test, protected-scope, conflict-behavior, review-route, migration-policy, self-modification-rule, agency-rights, material-usability, pre-effect-review, rollback, correction, reviewer-independence, evidence-transition, and non-claim-boundary records; it does not prove moral correctness, deployed alignment, rights usability, reviewer-independence quality, rollback execution, consent quality, support-state promotion, or runtime policy behavior.

records.formal_target: Protected agency rights remain available after an accepted transition.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Corrigibility as a finite agency-transition predicate: protected agency rights remain available after valid accepted transitions.

records.formal_target: A transition that removes a required correction pathway is rejected.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Corrigibility as a finite correction-pathway predicate: removing a required correction pathway requires rejection.

records.formal_target: A decision with unresolved protected conflicts carries a residual conflict record.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ValueConflict as a finite conflict-decision predicate: decisions with unresolved protected conflicts require residual conflict records.

records.formal_target: A high-stakes conflict cannot bypass the required review predicate.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ValueConflict as a finite high-stakes review predicate: a high-stakes conflict without required review cannot be accepted.

records.formal_target: Modeled value-conflict lifecycle admission routes missing conflict records, value axes, stakeholders, stakes, reversibility, authority boundaries, evidence requirements, review routes, high-stakes review, residual uncertainty, dissent preservation, authority narrowing, expiry/revisit records, evidence transitions, and non-claim boundaries to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ValueConflict as a finite value-conflict lifecycle route over conflict records, value axes, stakeholder records, stakes, reversibility, authority boundaries, evidence requirements, review routes, high-stakes review, residual uncertainty, dissent preservation, authority narrowing, expiry/revisit records, evidence-transition records, and non-claim boundaries; it does not prove moral correctness, classification quality, reviewer quality, deployed contestability, legal rights, support-state promotion, or runtime governance behavior.

records.formal_target: A governance transition preserves required audit and exit capabilities.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.GovernanceRights as a finite governance-transition predicate: accepted transitions preserve required audit and exit capabilities.

records.formal_target: A transition that removes a protected right is rejected or marked invalid.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.GovernanceRights as a finite protected-right predicate: removing a protected right requires rejection or invalid marking.

records.formal_target: An implementation can replace another only if it satisfies the field qualification predicate.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A replacement that expands authority without a governance grant is rejected.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A structured SCF lifecycle review and finite lifecycle-state transition relation route identity mismatch, missing evidence, stale leases, evaluator capture, authority expansion, open incidents, missing rollback, missing regression preservation, missing deprecation notice, and missing retirement receipt away from default or terminal promotion.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.StableCapabilityFields as finite lifecycle-review routing plus a lifecycle-state transition predicate over shadow, canary, qualified, default, deprecated, retired, and quarantined records with derived negative cases for missing evidence, regression floor, authority ceiling, rollback readiness, incident closure, deprecation notice, and retirement receipt.

records.formal_target: The synthetic SCF lifecycle trace summary records two valid traces, six expected-invalid controls, forward lifecycle coverage, incident quarantine coverage, unsafe-transition rejection, and no deployed-route-validation, rollback-execution, or support-state-promotion claim.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.StableCapabilityFields as a finite summary bridge for python3 scripts/validate_scf_lifecycle_trace.py; the bridge records trace counts, forward and quarantine coverage, negative controls, and explicit no-promotion boundaries.

records.formal_target: A replacement commit requires qualification evidence and rollback metadata.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A failed regression blocks promotion of the replacement.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: Structured replacement transaction and lifecycle reviews route missing artifacts, identity mismatch, authority expansion, evaluator capture, stale evidence, failed regression floors, missing canary scope, failed canaries, missing monitor windows, monitor incidents, missing rollback handles, missing rollback receipts, failed rollback dry runs, irreversible-effect ownership gaps, residual-owner gaps, deprecation/retirement gaps, and missing non-claim boundaries away from default promotion.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Replacement as finite transaction and lifecycle route functions with derived route theorems for missing artifacts, identity mismatch, authority expansion without approval, evaluator capture, stale evidence, failed regression floors, missing canary scope, failed canary, missing monitor windows, monitor incident, missing rollback handles, missing rollback receipts, failed rollback dry run, unowned irreversible effects, missing residual owners, deprecation notice gaps, retirement receipt gaps, missing non-claim boundaries, and complete default-review cases.

records.formal_target: The deterministic replacement trace probe records six trace steps, two valid synthetic transactions, three rejected negative controls, a canary kept non-default, a monitor-triggered rollback, rollback dry run, residuals, no support-state effect, and non-claim boundary.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Replacement as a finite synthetic trace-summary mirror: it records trace counts, canary non-default status, monitor-triggered rollback, rollback dry-run presence, rejected negative controls, residuals, no-support-effect status, and non-claim boundary only, without proving deployed replacement behavior, monitor quality, regression-suite quality, production rollback, or chapter-core support movement.

records.formal_target: A secret handle can be substituted only inside an authorized execution boundary.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A context packet with insufficient clearance cannot enter a protected SCIF.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A structured authority-use review routes missing handles, inactive leases, missing approvals, unauthorized boundaries, missing substitution permission, insufficient clearance, prompt injection, missing SCIFs, unsanitized output, residual leak risk, revocation requests, and clean authorized use into explicit security-kernel outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SecurityKernel as a finite authority-use route function and derived route theorems for missing handles, revocation, inactive leases, missing approvals, unauthorized boundaries, missing substitution permission, insufficient clearance, prompt injection, missing required SCIFs, unsanitized output, residual leak risk, and clean authorized use.

records.formal_target: An improvement transition preserves all protected invariants.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SelfImprovement as a finite improvement-transition predicate: accepted transitions preserve declared protected invariants.

records.formal_target: A proposal evaluated only by the component being replaced cannot be promoted.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SelfImprovement as a finite self-evaluation predicate: proposals evaluated only by the replaced component cannot be promoted.

records.formal_target: A structured self-improvement transition review routes missing proposals, missing invariant declarations, missing evidence bundles, invariant breaches, sole self-evaluation, missing independent evaluators, authority/security/resource boundary deltas, missing approval, missing rollback, stale gates, missing residual escrow, canary monitor failures, complete canary reviews, and complete promotion reviews into explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SelfImprovement as a finite transition-route function and derived route theorems for missing proposals, missing invariant declarations, missing evidence bundles, invariant breaches, sole self-evaluation, missing independent evaluators, authority/security/resource boundary deltas, missing approval, missing rollback, stale gates, missing residual escrow, canary monitor failures, complete canary reviews, and complete promotion reviews.

records.formal_target: A compiled execution job preserves the parent contract constraints.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.IntentToExecution as a finite execution-compilation predicate: declared parent constraints are preserved by a valid compilation review.

records.formal_target: An execution job without required approval cannot transition to running.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.IntentToExecution as a finite approval-review predicate: missing required approval blocks transition to running.

records.formal_target: A structured execution dispatch review routes missing contracts, missing objective fields, authority widening, hidden overrides, missing approvals, missing artifacts, missing verification plans, known residuals, and complete dispatch reviews into explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.IntentToExecution as a finite dispatch-route function and derived route theorems for missing contracts, missing objectives, authority widening, hidden overrides, missing approvals, missing artifacts, missing verification plans, known residuals, and complete dispatch reviews.

records.formal_target: A valid command contract contains objective, constraints, output contract, verification, and failure behavior.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CommandContracts as a finite required-field predicate: command contracts carry objective, constraints, output, verification, and failure behavior fields.

records.formal_target: A hidden or conflicting instruction cannot override an explicit contract constraint.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CommandContracts as a finite precedence predicate: hidden or conflicting instructions cannot be accepted as overriding explicit constraints.

records.formal_target: A structured command-field confidence review routes inferred or missing required field confidence away from dispatch while allowing complete confirmed/policy-imposed fields to dispatch.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CommandContracts as a finite field-confidence route: inferred authority confidence and missing output confidence require review while complete confirmed/policy-imposed confidence is dispatch-eligible.

records.formal_target: A plan node inherits the authority ceiling of its parent contract unless governance lowers it.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: A plan with unsatisfied required constraints cannot be dispatched.
records.rationale: Initial triage: this target can be stated over finite records, state machines, orderings, or transition predicates and is a credible Lean/executable-model candidate.

records.formal_target: Modeled plan-graph admission routes missing command-contract acceptance, decomposition, acyclicity, dependency order, authority inheritance, context demand, adequacy contracts, verification plans, dispatch gates, dispatch receipts, replanning controls, residual registers, and non-claim boundaries to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Planning as a finite plan-graph admission route function and derived route theorems for missing command-contract acceptance, decomposition, acyclicity, dependency order, authority inheritance, context demand, adequacy contracts, verification plans, dispatch gates, dispatch receipts, replanning controls, residual registers, non-claim boundaries, and complete new or replanned graph records.

records.formal_target: A dispatchable plan graph is acyclic and all dependencies precede dependents.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PlanForge as a finite indexed plan-graph predicate: dispatchable records carry an acyclicity certificate and all member dependency edges precede dependents.

records.formal_target: A node whose quality predicate fails must escalate or emit a residual.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PlanForge as a finite node-outcome predicate: a failed quality predicate must route to escalation or residual under ValidNodeOutcome.

records.formal_target: A compiled artifact preserves all required IR obligations.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CognitiveCompilation as a finite compiled-artifact predicate: required IR obligations are preserved by a valid compilation review.

records.formal_target: A repair that invalidates an existing obligation cannot be accepted without updating the ledger.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CognitiveCompilation as a finite repair-acceptance predicate: accepted repairs that invalidate existing obligations require ledger updates.

records.formal_target: A structured semantic-lowering review routes missing source plans, missing atoms, missing obligation links, cyclic dependencies, authority escapes, missing validators, validator failures, missing receipts, obligation loss, invalidating repairs without ledger updates, known residuals, and complete lowering reviews into explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CognitiveCompilation as a finite semantic-lowering route function and derived route theorems for missing source plans, missing atoms, missing obligation links, cyclic dependencies, authority escapes, missing validators, validator failures, missing receipts, obligation loss, invalidating repairs without ledger updates, known residuals, and complete lowering reviews.

records.formal_target: A context reference resolves only when the requested address and version are valid for the snapshot.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.VirtualContextABI as a finite context-reference predicate: resolved records require a matching address, version, and snapshot binding.

records.formal_target: A mandatory context miss produces a typed fault rather than a best-effort packet.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.VirtualContextABI as a finite lookup predicate: mandatory misses emit typed faults and no best-effort materialization.

records.formal_target: A structured context-admission review routes malformed requests, missing addresses, authority escapes, absent mandatory context, absent optional context, missing certificates, stale certificates, taint, failed adequacy, residuals, and complete materialization reviews into explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.VirtualContextABI as a finite context-admission route function and derived route theorems for malformed requests, missing addresses, authority escapes, absent mandatory context, absent optional context, missing certificates, stale certificates, taint, failed adequacy, residuals, and complete materialization reviews.

records.formal_target: A derived context cell carries source bindings and a declared loss/use contract.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates as a finite certificate predicate: valid derived cells declare source bindings, loss contracts, and permitted uses.

records.formal_target: A summary cannot increase the authority ceiling of its source cells.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates as a finite authority predicate: summaries respecting source cells cannot exceed source authority ceilings.

records.formal_target: A structured certificate-lifecycle review routes malformed certificates, missing source bindings, missing loss or use contracts, authority escapes, omitted-material gaps, scope violations, stale certificates, revocation, taint, deletion-closure gaps, missing verifier refs, consumer-policy violations, support-promotion attempts without evidence transitions, and complete reviews into explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ContextCertificates as a finite certificate-lifecycle route function and derived route theorems for malformed certificates, missing source bindings, missing loss or use contracts, authority escapes, omitted-material gaps, scope violations, stale certificates, revocation, taint, deletion-closure gaps, missing verifier refs, consumer-policy violations, support-promotion attempts without evidence transitions, and complete reviews.

records.formal_target: A snapshot read sees the committed events in its declared view.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ContextTransactions as a finite event/snapshot predicate: valid snapshot reads expose committed events in the declared view.

records.formal_target: A tainted source produces tainted derived context unless declassification is authorized.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ContextTransactions as a finite taint predicate: tainted sources taint derivatives unless declassification is authorized.

records.formal_target: A context packet admitted for use may still be marked inadequate for a target claim.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.VerificationBandwidth as a finite adequacy-state witness: context admission and verification adequacy are distinct states.

records.formal_target: A high-risk claim with inadequate context cannot receive a verified support label.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.VerificationBandwidth as a finite claim-support predicate: high-risk claims with inadequate context cannot receive verified support.

records.formal_target: A structured verification-adequacy review routes missing claims, unadmitted context, high-risk inadequate context, missing pairwise checks, missing verification artifacts, open negative evidence, contradictions, residuals, complete verified-support reviews, and complete draft-support reviews into explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.VerificationBandwidth as a finite verification-adequacy route function and derived route theorems for missing claims, unadmitted context, high-risk inadequate context, missing pairwise checks, missing verification artifacts, open negative evidence, contradictions, residuals, complete verified-support reviews, and complete draft-support reviews.

records.formal_target: A claim update preserves prior evidence and revision history.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ClaimLedger as a finite claim-update predicate: valid updates carry prior evidence and revision-history references forward.

records.formal_target: A contradiction prevents promotion until resolved, bounded, or recorded as residual.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ClaimLedger as a finite promotion-review predicate: open contradictions block promotion until handled.

records.formal_target: A modeled revision request routes missing claim identity, support-state gaps, unsupported promotion, open contradictions, history loss, surface-sync gaps, split/downgrade/residual gaps, and non-claim-boundary gaps to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ClaimLedger as a finite revision-lifecycle route function and derived route theorems for missing claim identity, missing support-state records, unsupported promotion, open contradictions, missing history, non-overwrite attestation gaps, surface-sync gaps, split-history gaps, downgrade-reason gaps, residual-record gaps, non-claim-boundary gaps, and complete revision acceptance.

records.formal_target: A claim at a formal support tier carries a valid proof or justification artifact reference.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ProofCarryingClaims as a finite tier predicate: formal support tiers require a valid justification artifact reference.

records.formal_target: A failed verifier result downgrades or blocks the claim rather than promoting it.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ProofCarryingClaims as a finite verifier-result predicate: failed verifier results require downgrade/block status and prevent promotion.

records.formal_target: A tribunal verdict includes reviewer roles, evidence references, and unresolved dissent.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Tribunal as a finite verdict predicate: tribunal verdicts require reviewer roles, evidence references, and unresolved dissent records.

records.formal_target: A high-risk artifact cannot be accepted when required tribunal review is absent.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Tribunal as a finite high-risk acceptance predicate: high-risk artifacts missing required tribunal review cannot be accepted.

records.formal_target: A job transitions only through valid lifecycle states.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.TypedJobs as a finite lifecycle predicate: transition records marked valid must use the declared lifecycle relation.

records.formal_target: A job requiring approval cannot execute before approval is recorded.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.TypedJobs as a finite approval predicate: approval-required jobs cannot be allowed to run when no approval record exists.

records.formal_target: A structured job-execution review routes missing jobs, unlocked contracts, invalid lifecycles, missing approvals, missing permissions, observed failures, residuals, unverified delivery, verified delivery, scheduler-slot waits, dispatch-ready jobs, and retirement requests into explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.TypedJobs as a finite job-execution route function and derived route theorems for missing jobs, unlocked contracts, invalid lifecycles, missing approvals, missing permissions, observed failures, residuals, unverified delivery, verified delivery, scheduler-slot waits, dispatch-ready jobs, and retirement requests.

records.formal_target: Every produced artifact records its parent job and source/context references.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph as a finite provenance predicate: produced artifact records expose parent-job and source/context references.

records.formal_target: An artifact with missing required provenance cannot support a promoted claim.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph as a finite promotion predicate: missing required provenance blocks promoted-claim support.

records.formal_target: A modeled artifact replay packet routes cross-record mismatches, missing observation, stale certificates, support-review transaction gaps, and partial-replay promotion attempts to explicit outcomes while admitting complete bounded-review packets.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactGraph as a finite replay-packet route bridge matching the synthetic artifact-graph replay harness shape for cross-record alignment, byte-exact observation, stale-certificate blocking, support-review transaction validation, partial-replay promotion blocking, record-only partial replay, and bounded-review admission.

records.formal_target: A runtime adapter invocation is valid only when job permissions include the adapter capability and its effect lease is active, scoped to that capability, and sandboxed.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.RuntimeAdapters as finite permission and effect-lease predicates: valid adapter invocations require parent job permission plus an active, capability-scoped, sandboxed effect lease.

records.formal_target: A high-impact adapter call without approval or a required rollback handle is rejected, mismatched, expired, or unsandboxed effect leases cannot validate invocation, and modeled confused-deputy or sandbox-escape attempts cannot pass dispatch.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.RuntimeAdapters as finite approval, lease, rollback-obligation, confused-deputy, sandbox-escape, and dispatch-route predicates: high-impact calls without approval or required rollback handles are rejected, mismatched, expired, or unsandboxed leases cannot validate invocation, and modeled confused-deputy or sandbox-escape attempts cannot pass dispatch.

records.formal_target: A generated tool records its source traces, parameters, and verification result.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ProceduralMemory as a finite generated-tool predicate: generated tools require source traces, parameters, and verification-result records.

records.formal_target: A tool with failed regression cannot be promoted to routable status.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ProceduralMemory as a finite regression predicate: tools with failed regressions cannot be promoted to routable status.

records.formal_target: A router may select only specialists whose authority and readiness satisfy the task requirement.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Routing as a finite route-selection predicate: selected routes satisfy authority and readiness.

records.formal_target: A failed readiness predicate routes to fallback or residual, not promotion.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Routing as a finite readiness-failure predicate: failed readiness routes to fallback or residual and is not promoted.

records.formal_target: A modeled routing decision routes missing requests, registry gaps, authority mismatches, readiness failures, stale leases, missing cost-quality records, overprivileged selections, missing rejected-candidate evidence, residual ownership gaps, and missing non-claim boundaries to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.Routing as a finite routing-decision lifecycle route function and derived route theorems for missing capability requests, missing specialist registries, authority mismatches, readiness failures, fallback and residual handling, missing leases, missing cost-quality records, overprivileged selections, missing rejected-candidate evidence, residual-owner gaps, missing non-claim boundaries, and complete route selection.

records.formal_target: A module can enter promoted or stronger readiness states only after required gates and finite lifecycle records for fresh evidence, residual escrow, fallback, expiry, regression floor, authority scope, and route permission are present.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ReadinessGates as finite promotion and lifecycle-transition predicates: promoted decisions require all required gates, and stronger readiness/default transitions require fresh evidence, residual escrow, fallback, expiry, regression, authority, and route records where applicable.

records.formal_target: A quarantined, superseded, retired, stale, or incomplete readiness transition cannot be used for ordinary promotion without the required fallback, residual, rerun, supersession, or retirement records.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ReadinessGates as finite quarantine, stale-gate, supersession, retirement, and lifecycle-transition negative cases: quarantined targets cannot be ordinary routed, stale gates require rerun/residual records, supersession and retirement require explicit records, and retired states cannot emit allowed transitions.

records.formal_target: A runtime core promotion requires readiness, regression, and replay evidence references.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.MoECOTRuntime as a finite runtime-core predicate: runtime core promotion requires readiness, regression, and replay evidence references.

records.formal_target: A runtime claim sourced only from unavailable text cannot be promoted above argument state.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.MoECOTRuntime as a finite source-boundary predicate: runtime claims sourced only from unavailable text cannot be promoted above argument state.

records.formal_target: A hive scheduler admits a job only when identity, data, tool, federation, and approval policy checks pass before optimization.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PersonalComputeHives as a finite admission predicate: admitted hive jobs require identity, data, tool, federation, and approval checks.

records.formal_target: A faster node cannot be selected when its policy membrane forbids the job's data, authority, or network scope.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PersonalComputeHives as a finite policy-first predicate: a faster node flagged as policy-forbidden cannot be selected.

records.formal_target: A compact representation with unresolved obligations carries residual records.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CompactGenerativeSystems as a finite residual-honesty predicate: unresolved obligations require residual records.

records.formal_target: A lossy representation cannot be marked exact without verification evidence.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CompactGenerativeSystems as a finite exactness predicate: lossy representations without verification evidence cannot be marked exact.

records.formal_target: A modeled compact-generation admission review routes missing source artifacts, compression boundaries, residual records, lossy exactness overclaims, reconstruction evidence, fallback paths, verifier-cost records, semantic provenance, hierarchy-migration records, evidence-transition gaps, and non-claim-boundary gaps to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CompactGenerativeSystems as a finite admission-route function and derived route theorems for missing source artifacts, compression boundaries, residual records, lossy exactness overclaims, reconstruction evidence, fallback paths, verifier-cost records, semantic provenance, hierarchy-migration records, support-promotion attempts without evidence transitions, missing non-claim boundaries, and complete compact-representation admission.

records.formal_target: The compact GVR synthetic slice has a finite Lean fixture bridge matching the literal baseline, selected compact receipt, lossy exactness control, negative-rate/no-fallback control, bounded-search-overrun control, and selected-versus-baseline byte relation.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CompactGenerativeSystems as a finite fixture bridge for the compact GVR synthetic slice: the selected repeat-generator-plus-repair receipt is eligible, lossy exactness, negative-rate/no-fallback, and bounded-search-overrun controls are ineligible, and the selected modeled byte count is below the literal baseline.

records.formal_target: An exact reconstruction claim requires generator output plus repair residual to equal the target.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.GenerateVerifyRepair as a finite reconstruction predicate: exact claims require generator output plus repair residual to equal the target.

records.formal_target: A failed verification blocks exactness promotion.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.GenerateVerifyRepair as a finite verification predicate: failed verification blocks exactness promotion.

records.formal_target: A fast generation route records generation mode, verifier, acceptance predicate, risk tier, and fallback before promotion.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.FastGeneration as a finite route-record predicate: promotion requires generation mode, verifier, acceptance predicate, risk tier, and fallback fields.

records.formal_target: Raw tokens per second cannot promote a fast generation claim without accepted or verified token evidence, task-success evidence, and a baseline.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.FastGeneration as a finite speed-evidence predicate: raw token-speed claims cannot promote when accepted-output, task-success, or baseline evidence is missing.

records.formal_target: A modeled fast-generation admission review routes missing mode records, context packets, risk tiers, quality targets, verifiers, acceptance predicates, baselines, accepted outputs, verifier costs, fallback and residual handling, high-risk override gaps, budget gaps, evidence-transition gaps, and non-claim-boundary gaps to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.FastGeneration as a finite admission-lifecycle route function and derived route theorems for missing mode records, context packets, risk tiers, quality targets, verifiers, acceptance predicates, baselines, accepted outputs, verifier costs, failed-acceptance fallback and residual handling, high-risk override gaps, slow verified fallback gaps, budget gaps, evidence-transition gaps, non-claim-boundary gaps, and complete fast-mode admission.

records.formal_target: The public Project Theseus generation-mode import fixture matches a finite Lean summary fixture and proves the imported accepted-span speed-lift warnings do not permit promotion without useful-solution evidence, promotable comparisons, all hard boundary gates passing, and zero missing report refs.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.FastGeneration and checked by scripts/validate_theseus_generation_mode_import.py as a finite fixture bridge over the public static import summary. It aligns the JSON mode/comparison/no-promotion counts, all-gates-passed boundary, and zero missing-report-ref boundary with Lean constants and proves only the no-promotion/gate-completeness boundary; it does not prove generation speed, useful-solution-per-second improvement, model quality, benchmark quality, or a live Project Theseus replay.

records.formal_target: A compressed artifact used for a task must pass that task probe or route to fallback.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactCompression as a finite artifact-use predicate: task use requires a passed task probe or fallback routing.

records.formal_target: A compression record cannot omit residual or fallback metadata.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactCompression as a finite metadata-completeness predicate: promotion requires residual and fallback metadata.

records.formal_target: A modeled compressed-artifact admission review routes missing preserved full artifacts, missing manifests, use-envelope and access-pattern gaps, unadmitted states, decoder-determinism gaps, exact-replay readiness failures, failed probes, missing fallback artifacts, residual metadata gaps, utility-evidence gaps, support-promotion gaps, and missing non-claim boundaries to explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactCompression as a finite admission-lifecycle route function and derived route theorems for missing preserved full artifacts, missing manifests, use-envelope and access-pattern gaps, unadmitted states, decoder-determinism gaps, exact-replay readiness failures, failed probes, fallback-artifact gaps, residual-metadata gaps, utility-evidence gaps, missing evidence-transition records for support-promotion requests, missing non-claim boundaries, and complete compressed-artifact admission.

records.formal_target: A semantic node marked grounded has at least one provenance link.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SemanticRepresentation as a finite semantic-node predicate: grounded nodes require a positive provenance-link count.

records.formal_target: A hierarchy update preserves prior node references or records supersession.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SemanticRepresentation as a finite hierarchy-update predicate: applied updates preserve prior references or record supersession.

records.formal_target: A task budget cannot disable required safety or verification gates.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ResourceEconomics as a finite required-gate predicate: required safety and verification gates cannot be disabled by a budget decision.

records.formal_target: A high-risk task with insufficient verification budget is blocked or escalated.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ResourceEconomics as a finite risk/budget predicate: high-risk work with insufficient verification budget must route away from ordinary dispatch.

records.formal_target: The four-route costed-route fixture rejects the failed-verification and hidden-residual controls, keeps the bounded transform route eligible, proves it is lowest-cost among eligible modeled routes, and proves a finite selector-state replay ends with that route after rejecting the cheaper controls.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ResourceEconomics as a finite bridge over the public four-route costed-route fixture: it proves the bounded route is eligible, the failed-verification and hidden-residual controls are rejected, the bounded route is lowest-cost among modeled eligible routes, and the tracked result record exposes the Python/Lean field alignment for route constructors, costs, eligibility booleans, negative controls, and selector-trace expectations. It does not prove deployed routing, scheduler behavior, workload quality, economic optimality, or chapter-core support-state promotion.

records.formal_target: The finite Resource workflow trace fixture carries dispatch events whose costs, review minutes, and verification minutes roll up to the public summary, whose schedule keeps high-risk release work before lower-risk work, whose selected events preserve protected-overhead, residual-ownership, and non-claim guard flags, and whose public negative controls reject over-budget aggregate resource bills.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ResourceEconomics as a finite dispatch-event bridge over the public Resource workflow trace: it proves fixture event costs, review minutes, and verification minutes roll up to the tracked summary, preserves high-risk-first order, preserves protected-overhead, residual-ownership, and non-claim guard flags, and records the over-budget aggregate-resource-bill rejection in the public summary bridge. It does not prove deployed scheduler behavior, live workload quality, physical feasibility, economic outcomes, or chapter-core support-state promotion.

records.formal_target: The finite capacity-smoothing reviewer trace fixture preserves bounded capacity, reviewer capacity, protected review overhead, displaced-review-cost residualization, no low-risk review during blocked protected review, and no support-state promotion; negative cases reject low-risk review hoarding, high-risk review without protected overhead, and missing displaced-cost residuals.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ResourceEconomics as a finite reviewer-capacity bridge over the public capacity-smoothing fixture: it preserves bounded capacity, reviewer capacity arithmetic, protected review overhead, displaced-review-cost residualization, no low-risk review while protected review is blocked, and no support-state promotion. It does not prove live scheduler behavior, real reviewer-load stability, production throughput, workload quality, or chapter-core support-state promotion.

records.formal_target: Aggregate serving-throughput or KV-cache reuse claims remain valid only when KV-cache budget, batching scope, and single-request verified-output boundaries are recorded; throughput-to-quality overclaims reject validity.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ResourceEconomics as a finite serving-memory accounting predicate: aggregate throughput claims require recorded KV-cache budget, batching scope, and single-request verified-output separation, and throughput-to-quality overclaims reject validity. It does not prove KV-cache behavior, serving throughput, single-request quality, model quality, workload quality, or chapter-core support-state promotion.

records.formal_target: A simulation claim includes declared scope, fidelity, and resource bounds.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SimulationFidelity as a finite claim-field predicate: simulation claims used as evidence require declared scope, fidelity, and resource bounds. Folded into Resource Economics and Token Budgets on 2026-06-30 as the Simulation Fidelity and Claim Transport proof lane.

records.formal_target: An experiment result cannot exceed the declared fidelity support of its simulation.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SimulationFidelity as a finite fidelity-support predicate: promoted experiment results cannot exceed declared support level. Folded into Resource Economics and Token Budgets on 2026-06-30 as the Simulation Fidelity and Claim Transport proof lane.

records.formal_target: A substrate adoption record includes baseline, measured target, and falsification criterion.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SearchSubstrates as a finite record-field predicate: substrate adoption records expose baseline, measured-target, and falsification fields before adoption claims are reviewed.

records.formal_target: A substrate without passing evidence remains non-core.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.SearchSubstrates as a finite promotion predicate: a substrate without passing evidence remains in a non-core adoption state.

records.formal_target: A proof-carrying AI contract exposes theorem references, deterministic fields, and an explicit non-claim boundary before downstream use.
records.rationale: Implemented as a local finite-record Lean predicate requiring theorem references, deterministic fields, and an explicit non-claim boundary before downstream use; it does not resolve external Circle theorem IDs.

records.formal_target: A downstream claim cannot be promoted solely from contract readiness without a workload, baseline, metric, and evidence artifact.
records.rationale: Implemented as a local finite-record Lean consumer-gate predicate requiring workload, baseline, metric, and evidence artifacts beyond contract readiness before downstream promotion.

records.formal_target: A cyclic memory claim records residue and winding or marks aliasing as visible residual risk.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CoilAttentionMemory as a finite alias-boundary predicate: reused cyclic slots require residue/winding records or visible alias residuals.

records.formal_target: Sparse coverage or freshness facts alone cannot promote a retrieval-quality claim.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CoilAttentionMemory as a finite retrieval-quality predicate: sparse coverage and freshness facts without semantic-quality evidence cannot promote retrieval quality.

records.formal_target: A cyclic mixer claim records the structural invariant separately from quality, runtime, memory, and parameter claims.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CyclicMixers as a finite claim-separation predicate: structural invariants are separated from quality, runtime, memory, and parameter claims.

records.formal_target: A cyclic substrate cannot be promoted without ordinary baselines and recorded tradeoff metrics.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.CyclicMixers as a finite promotion predicate: promoted cyclic substrates require baseline references and tradeoff metrics.

records.formal_target: A proof target marked implemented has an existing module and passes the build.
records.rationale: Implemented as a local finite-record Lean predicate requiring module-existence and build-passed fields for implemented proof-target status; repository filesystem checks remain in validation scripts.

records.formal_target: A proof target for a non-operational claim remains planned or blocked, not implemented.
records.rationale: Implemented as a local finite-record Lean predicate routing non-operational proof targets to planned or blocked status rather than implemented status.

records.formal_target: A capability promotion requires benchmark evidence and preserved regression records.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.BenchmarkRatchets as a finite capability-promotion predicate: promotion requires benchmark evidence and preserved regression records.

records.formal_target: A saturated benchmark cannot be the sole basis for higher readiness promotion.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.BenchmarkRatchets as a finite saturation predicate: a saturated benchmark alone cannot promote higher readiness.

records.formal_target: The benchmark anti-Goodhart fixture bridge mirrors 2 valid fixtures, 5 expected-invalid controls, promotion-ready, regression-floor, missing-checks, blocked-ratchet policy, reward-as-truth, saturated-promotion, release-approval, no-support-promotion, and non-claim-boundary facts.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.BenchmarkRatchets as a finite fixture-summary mirror for the benchmark anti-Goodhart harness: it records positive/negative synthetic controls and no-support-promotion boundaries only, without proving empirical benchmark quality, hidden-holdout integrity, policy-training quality, steward execution, release safety, Theseus benchmark practice, or chapter-core support movement.

records.formal_target: An admitted policy update records target layer, reward signal, update constraint, evaluation refs, governance gates, and rollback plan.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PolicyOptimization as a finite policy-update predicate: admitted policy updates require target-layer, reward-signal, update-constraint, evaluation, governance-gate, and rollback fields.

records.formal_target: A policy update with unverified reward or missing governance gate cannot be promoted.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PolicyOptimization as a finite reward/governance predicate: unverified reward or missing governance gate blocks policy-update promotion.

records.formal_target: A policy-promotion route rejects or routes updates with inadmissible feedback, missing target evaluation, missing holdout or contamination checks, missing reward-hacking probes, governance or authority gaps, missing rollback, or regression/residual gaps before promotion.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PolicyOptimization as a finite promotion-route predicate: inadmissible feedback, missing target evaluation, missing holdout or contamination checks, missing reward-hacking probes, governance or authority gaps, missing rollback, and regression/residual gaps route away from promotion.

records.formal_target: The deterministic policy-update lease probe records six synthetic routing samples, five candidate policies, selected canary kept experimental, expected-invalid controls, rollback dry run, no support-state effect, and non-claim boundary.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PolicyOptimization as a finite synthetic lease-probe summary: it mirrors sample/candidate counts, expected-invalid control rejection, rollback dry-run presence, no-support-effect status, and non-claim boundary only, without proving optimizer behavior, reward quality, route quality, live canary behavior, live rollback behavior, or chapter-core support movement.

records.formal_target: A steward-managed work contract records objective, authority, allowed tools, forbidden tools, verification requirements, budget, and non-claims before dispatch.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactStewardAgents as a finite work-contract predicate: dispatched contracts require objective, authority, allowed tools, forbidden tools, verification requirements, budget, and non-claims.

records.formal_target: A steward action that exceeds treasury policy, changes governance rules, or touches protected assets cannot execute without explicit approval evidence.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactStewardAgents as a finite protected-action predicate: treasury-exceeding, governance-changing, or protected-asset actions without approval evidence cannot execute.

records.formal_target: An end-to-end trace contains required artifacts for each layer handoff.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ReferenceArchitecture as a finite handoff predicate: declared layer handoffs require associated artifacts.

records.formal_target: A trace with a missing governance gate cannot be marked valid.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ReferenceArchitecture as a finite governance-gate predicate: traces missing required governance gates cannot be marked valid.

records.formal_target: An implementation-reference claim names the report, config, or tool surface and does not rely on dashboard prose alone.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.TheseusReference as a finite implementation-reference predicate: reference claims require report, config, or tool surfaces and cannot rely on dashboard prose alone.

records.formal_target: A capability or self-evolution promotion is blocked when required gate reports are absent or failing.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.TheseusReference as a finite gate predicate: capability or self-evolution promotion is blocked without present passing gate reports.

records.formal_target: A roadmap phase can unlock a dependent phase only after acceptance gates pass.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PrototypeRoadmap as a finite phase-unlock predicate: dependent phases require declared passing acceptance gates before unlock.

records.formal_target: A phase milestone cannot promote a claim without evidence artifacts.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PrototypeRoadmap as a finite claim-promotion predicate: a phase milestone cannot promote a claim without evidence artifacts.

records.formal_target: Every chapter in the manifest has outline proof targets and generated claim placeholders.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.LivingBook as a finite chapter-manifest predicate: manifest chapters require outline proof targets and generated claim placeholders.

records.formal_target: A structural update without regenerated scaffold/proof manifest is invalid.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.LivingBook as a finite structural-update predicate: valid structural updates require regenerated scaffold and proof manifest.

records.formal_target: A source-derived claim requires a source note or equivalent ingested-source artifact.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.BibliographyPlan as a finite source-ingestion predicate: source-derived claims require a source note or equivalent ingested source artifact.

records.formal_target: A new source cannot be assigned to a non-existent chapter id.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.BibliographyPlan as a finite source-assignment predicate: accepted new-source assignments require an existing chapter id.

records.formal_target: A high-risk hive job that requires approval cannot execute unless a bound approval receipt is present.
records.rationale: Added with the personal-hive protocol expansion: the target is a finite record implication over high-risk job, approval-required, execution, and bound-receipt fields.

records.formal_target: External hive access requires an active lease with scope, sandbox, evidence, expiration, and revocation records.
records.rationale: Added with the H2H protocol expansion: the target is a finite record implication over external access, active lease, scope, sandbox, evidence, expiration, and revocation fields.

records.formal_target: A structured hive-work admission review routes malformed jobs, missing identity/data/tool policy, registry gaps, scheduler-policy gaps, high-risk jobs without portal approval, external access without leases or sandbox records, missing cost or energy budgets, missing dropout plans, missing audit receipt plans, missing residual owners, and support-promotion attempts without evidence transitions into explicit outcomes.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.PersonalComputeHives as a finite hive-work admission route function and derived route theorems for malformed jobs, missing policy checks, registry and scheduler-policy gaps, high-risk jobs without approval, external access lease or sandbox gaps, cost and energy budget gaps, missing dropout plans, missing audit receipt plans, missing residual owners, and support-promotion attempts without evidence transitions.

records.formal_target: A stewarded release publication requires test, evidence, changelog, residual, and approval records.
records.rationale: Added with the steward lifecycle expansion: the target is a finite release-gate implication over publication and required evidence records.

records.formal_target: When sunset criteria are met, ordinary work generation is blocked until a sunset review is opened.
records.rationale: Added with the steward lifecycle expansion: the target is a finite record implication blocking ordinary work when sunset criteria are met and no review is open.

records.formal_target: A steward lifecycle route sends tainted unreviewed events to quarantine, sunset criteria without review to sunset review, autonomy escalation without charter approval to approval, and over-policy treasury spend to approval.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactStewardAgents as a finite lifecycle-route decision function: tainted unreviewed events route to quarantine, sunset gaps route to sunset review, autonomy escalation without charter approval routes to approval, and over-policy treasury spend routes to approval.

records.formal_target: A steward contribution ledger keeps authorship, review, evidence, compensation, reputation, governance effect, and conflicts separated; collapsed governance scoring is rejected and support-state changes require evidence-transition records.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactStewardAgents as finite contribution-ledger route theorems: missing separated credits route to repair, collapsed governance scoring is rejected, support-state changes without transition evidence route to evidence review, and complete non-promoting ledgers can be accepted.

records.formal_target: A steward federation contract requires scoped work contracts, bounded worker authority, tool/data/budget gates, external-spend approval, and evidence-bundle requirements before dispatch.
records.rationale: Implemented in AsiStackProofs.ArtifactStewardAgents as finite federation-contract route theorems: missing contracts request repair, inherited project authority is rejected, external spend without approval routes to approval, and complete scoped federation dispatch requires evidence-bundle boundaries.
